1	# Analogy: monoidality of `pullbackComp` (D3′ Sq2b) — δ-transport across a left-adjoint composition iso
2	
3	## Mode
4	cross-domain-inspiration
5	
6	## Slug
7	ana258-d3
8	
9	## Iteration
10	258
11	
12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	We have two adjunctions `adj₀₁ : F₀₁ ⊣ G₁₀`, `adj₁₂ : F₁₂ ⊣ G₂₁` whose right adjoints `Gᵢ` are
14	lax-monoidal (so the left adjoints `Fᵢ` carry the canonical *oplax* structure, δ = mate of μ). We
15	also have a single adjunction `adj₀₂ : F₀₂ ⊣ G₂₀` and a **monoidal** natural iso of the right
16	adjoints `e₀₁₂ : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀` (here `pushforwardComp`). This induces the left-adjoint
17	composition iso `c := leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e₀₁₂ : F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ≅ F₀₂` (here
18	`pullbackComp`). The structural claim ("Sq2b") is that **`c` is a monoidal natural iso** between the
19	oplax functors `(F₀₁⋙F₁₂, comp_δ)` and `(F₀₂, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal)`:
20	```
21	δ(F₀₂) M N = c.inv.app (M⊗N) ≫ δ(F₀₁⋙F₁₂) M N ≫ (c.hom.app M ⊗ₘ c.hom.app N)
22	```
23	where `δ(F₀₁⋙F₁₂) = comp_δ = F₁₂.map (δ F₀₁ M N) ≫ δ F₁₂ _ _`.
24	
25	## Failed approaches (from directive)
26	- iter-256 "mirror `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` for the WHOLE `pullbackTensorMap`": disproven — the
27	  full `pullbackTensorMap` is a hand-built 4-fold composite (sheafification + δ + sheafifyTensorUnit
28	  + pullbackValIso), NOT an adjunction transpose, so there is no `homEquiv` bridge for it.
29	  **NOTE: this objection does NOT apply to Sq2b.** Sq2b is only the inner δ-transport across
30	  `pullbackComp`, and *that* δ IS an adjunction transpose (`leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` defines
31	  δ := homEquiv.symm(…)). The mirror is valid here.
32	- iter-257 "state the lemma directly with bare `δ (pullback (toRingCatSheafHom f).hom)`": three
33	  frictions — (1) `forget₂` monoidal-instance metavars stuck; (2) HO-unification of a standalone δ's
34	  `pullback` against `pullbackComp`'s pinned `(F := …⋙…)` codomain through the `(F⋙G).op⋙T` defeq;
35	  (3) ring-map reconcile `rfl` not firing during `≫`/`.app`. **All three are statement-shape
36	  artifacts of working at the Scheme/`forget₂` level instead of the PresheafOfModules level.**

38	## Analogues found
39	
40	### Analogue: `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` + `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean:1009`, simp lemma at :1063)
41	- **Domain**: category theory — monoidal/adjunction mate calculus (the project's own area, but the
42	  *abstract* layer, one shelf up from `PresheafOfModules`).
43	- **Same structural problem there**: defines the oplax δ of a left adjoint as the **mate** of the
44	  lax μ of its right adjoint: `δ F X Y := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm ((unit X ⊗ₘ unit Y) ≫ μ G X Y)`.
45	  Hence `homEquiv (δ F X Y) = (unit X ⊗ₘ unit Y) ≫ μ G X Y` — the canonical bridge that converts any
46	  δ-equation into a μ-equation on the right-adjoint side.
47	- **Technique**: `apply (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective`, then rewrite the LHS δ with
48	  `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ` (+ `Equiv.apply_symm_apply`) to land on `(unit⊗unit)≫μ`.
49	- **Mapping to project**: the project's `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal` (TensorObjSubstrate.lean:1146)
50	  IS `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` of `PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ'`. So
51	  `δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')` is exactly this Mathlib δ, and `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ` is
52	  the bridge — no project-specific `…homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` lemma is needed (that one
53	  existed only because `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` was a project def; `δ` is a Mathlib def).
54	- **Porting cost**: low. The bridge is already in Mathlib.

10	/-!
11	# Compatibilities for left adjoints from compatibilities satisfied by right adjoints
12	
13	In this file, given isomorphisms between compositions of right adjoint functors,
14	we obtain isomorphisms between the corresponding compositions of the left adjoint functors,
15	and show that the left adjoint functors satisfy properties similar to the left/right
16	unitality and the associativity of pseudofunctors if the right adjoint functors
17	satisfy the corresponding properties.
18	
19	This is used in `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback` to study
20	the behaviour with respect to composition of the pullback functors on presheaves
21	of modules, by reducing these definitions and properties to the (obvious) case of the
22	pushforward functors. Similar results are obtained for sheaves of modules
23	in `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	namespace CategoryTheory
30	
31	variable {C₀ C₁ C₂ C₃ : Type*} [Category* C₀] [Category* C₁] [Category* C₂] [Category* C₃]
32	
33	open Functor

35	namespace Adjunction
36

39	variable {F : C₀ ⥤ C₀} {G : C₀ ⥤ C₀} (adj : F ⊣ G) (e : G ≅ 𝟭 C₀)
40	
41	/-- If a right adjoint functor is isomorphic to the identity functor,
42	so is the left adjoint. -/
43	@[simps! -isSimp]
44	def leftAdjointIdIso {F : C₀ ⥤ C₀} {G : C₀ ⥤ C₀} (adj : F ⊣ G) (e : G ≅ 𝟭 C₀) :
45	    F ≅ 𝟭 C₀ := (conjugateIsoEquiv .id adj).symm e.symm
46	
47	@[simp]
48	lemma conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointIdIso_hom :
49	    conjugateEquiv .id adj (leftAdjointIdIso adj e).hom = e.inv := by
50	  simp [leftAdjointIdIso]
51	
52	end

54	section
55	
56	variable {F₀₁ : C₀ ⥤ C₁} {F₁₂ : C₁ ⥤ C₂} {F₀₂ : C₀ ⥤ C₂}
57	  {G₁₀ : C₁ ⥤ C₀} {G₂₁ : C₂ ⥤ C₁} {G₂₀ : C₂ ⥤ C₀}
58	  (adj₀₁ : F₀₁ ⊣ G₁₀) (adj₁₂ : F₁₂ ⊣ G₂₁) (adj₀₂ : F₀₂ ⊣ G₂₀)
59	
60	/-- A natural transformation `G₂₀ ⟶ G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀` involving right adjoint functors
61	induces a natural transformation `F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ⟶ F₀₂` between the corresponding
62	left adjoint functors. -/
63	@[simps! -isSimp]
64	def leftAdjointCompNatTrans (τ₀₁₂ : G₂₀ ⟶ G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀) :
65	    F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ⟶ F₀₂ :=
66	  (conjugateEquiv adj₀₂ (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂)).symm τ₀₁₂
67	
68	/-- A natural isomorphism `G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀` involving right adjoint functors
69	induces a natural isomorphism `F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ≅ F₀₂` between the corresponding
70	left adjoint functors. -/
71	@[simps! -isSimp]
72	def leftAdjointCompIso (e₀₁₂ : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :
73	    F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ≅ F₀₂ :=
74	  (conjugateIsoEquiv adj₀₂ (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂)).symm e₀₁₂.symm
75	
76	lemma leftAdjointCompIso_hom (e₀₁₂ : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :
77	    (leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e₀₁₂).hom =
78	      leftAdjointCompNatTrans adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e₀₁₂.inv :=
79	  rfl

82	lemma conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv (e₀₁₂ : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :
83	    conjugateEquiv (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂) adj₀₂
84	      (leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e₀₁₂).inv = e₀₁₂.hom := by
85	  dsimp only [leftAdjointCompIso]

95	    leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₁' adj₀₁ e₀₁₁' =
96	      isoWhiskerLeft _ (leftAdjointIdIso adj₁₁' e₁'₁) ≪≫ rightUnitor F₀₁ := by
97	  subst h

100	    ← Functor.map_comp_assoc, -Functor.map_comp]

103	    {F₀₀' : C₀ ⥤ C₀} {F₀'₁ : C₀ ⥤ C₁} {G₀'₀ : C₀ ⥤ C₀} {G₁₀' : C₁ ⥤ C₀}
104	    (adj₀₀' : F₀₀' ⊣ G₀'₀) (adj₀'₁ : F₀'₁ ⊣ G₁₀')
105	    (e₀₀'₁ : G₁₀' ⋙ G₀'₀ ≅ G₁₀') (e₀'₀ : G₀'₀ ≅ 𝟭 _)
106	    (h : e₀₀'₁ = isoWhiskerLeft G₁₀' e₀'₀ ≪≫ rightUnitor G₁₀') :
107	    leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₀' adj₀'₁ adj₀'₁ e₀₀'₁ =
108	      isoWhiskerRight (leftAdjointIdIso adj₀₀' e₀'₀) F₀'₁ ≪≫ leftUnitor F₀'₁ := by
109	  subst h

112	  have h₂ := congr_map (F₀₀' ⋙ F₀'₁) (e₀'₀.inv.naturality (adj₀'₁.unit.app X₀))
113	  simp only [id_obj, comp_obj, Functor.id_map, Functor.comp_map, Functor.map_comp] at h₁ h₂

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean — iter-259 (D3′ Sq2b)

## Objective
D3′ Sq2b → `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`. Per the directive: add the standalone presheaf-level
**Sq2b** lemma (monoidality of `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp`) by mirroring the compiling
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` with η→δ; then attempt the full `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`.

## Result: Sq2b mate-calculus REDUCTION COMPLETE; isolated to ONE genuine residual.

Two new declarations added before `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`:

### `pullbackComp_δ` (Sq2b) — **FULLY PROVEN** (axiom-clean modulo the residual below)
The presheaf-level δ-transport across `pullbackComp`:
`δ (pullback (φ ≫ F.op ◁ ψ)) M N = c.inv (M⊗N) ≫ δ(pullback φ ⋙ pullback ψ) M N ≫ (c.hom M ⊗ₘ c.hom N)`.
`lean_verify` → axioms `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (sorryAx ONLY via the
residual `pushforwardComp_lax_μ`; no custom axioms). The complete ~90-line mate-calculus proof
compiles. Every mathematical step fired:
- transpose under `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (φ≫F.op◁ψ)).homEquiv.injective`;
- LHS δ via `Adjunction.unit_app_tensor_comp_map_δ` (the mate-of-μ identity);
- (MATE) `hconj`/`hmate`: conjugate of `pullbackComp.inv` is `pushforwardComp.hom = 𝟙`
  (`conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv`, `unit_conjugateEquiv`; `pushforwardComp = Iso.refl`);
- (U-C) `unit_app_tensor_comp_map_δ` for the composite adjunction `aφ.comp aψ`;
- (μ-COH) rewrite via `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` (← the residual);
- (μ-NAT) `Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural`;
- (TRI) `htri`: `aC.unit P ≫ map(c.hom P) = aχ.unit P` from `hmate` + `inv_hom_id_app`;
- tensorHom merge + `Category.assoc`.

### `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` — **THE GENUINE RESIDUAL** (typed `sorry`, sectionwise-reduced)
`μ(pushforward ψ ⋙ pushforward φ) X Y = μ(pushforward (φ ≫ F.op ◁ ψ)) X Y`.
This is the "pushforwardComp is monoidal" theorem (right-adjoint side of Sq2b). **Empirically NOT
`rfl`** and `simp`-resistant — directly contradicting the `d3sq2b258` recipe's prediction that this
residual would be "rfl/short ext (cf. the rfl-closed `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`)". The η-twin
was rfl because η only touches ε; the μ-version is the full tensorator interchange. Body advances to
the sectionwise `ModuleCat` leaf (`ext W x`); the leaf is a `ModuleCat.extendScalars`/`restrictScalars`
base-change associativity coherence for the composite ring hom (`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp`,
`ModuleCat.homEquiv_extendScalarsComp`, `ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp`) — a ~150-LOC ModuleCat
change-of-rings build. This is the REVERSING SIGNAL the directive armed: a step with no analog in the
compiling `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`.

## Summary
- **Sorry count (bare `sorry`): 2 → 3.** Net +1, but this is genuine DECOMPOSITION: the entire Sq2b
  mate calculus is now PROVEN (`pullbackComp_δ`), with the single genuine obstacle isolated and
  precisely stated (`pushforwardComp_lax_μ`).
- Closed: none of the pre-existing sorries; instead `pullbackComp_δ` (Sq2b) added FULLY PROVEN
  modulo the residual.
- Open (mine): `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` (genuine ModuleCat coherence, sectionwise-reduced).
- Untouched: `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L715, off-limits); `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` (L2392 —
  the 4-square assembly Sq1/Sq3/Sq4 NOT done; `pullbackComp_δ` is the ready Sq2 ingredient).
- D1′/D2′ kept GREEN (not touched).

## Why I stopped
**Real progress (code):** `pullbackComp_δ` is a complete, compiling ~90-line mate-calculus proof
(verified step-by-step via `lean_goal`), reducing Sq2b to exactly one residual. This validates the
analogist's reduction skeleton end-to-end and isolates the true gap. The residual
`pushforwardComp_lax_μ` is the genuine new obstacle: I empirically refuted the recipe's "rfl/short
ext" claim (tested `rfl`, `ext W x; rfl`, `ext W x; simp`, `dsimp [...]; rfl` — all fail; the leaf is
the explicit `extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` base-change expression). Per the armed reversing
signal, I left the typed `sorry` (sectionwise-reduced) + this report rather than stacking a new
abstract layer.

Not attempted: closing `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` (the ~150-LOC ModuleCat coherence — out of budget, and
the informal agent is UNAVAILABLE: the only key set, MOONSHOT, returns 401 invalid-auth); the 4-square
assembly of `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` (gated on Sq1/Sq3/Sq4 + this residual).

## Reusable tactic recipes discovered (for next iter / memory)
- **Statement shape**: state Sq2b's LHS pullback as `pullback (F := F⋙G) (R := T₀⋙forget₂) (φ ≫
  F.op.whiskerLeft ψ)` — pinning BOTH `F` and `R` is required so (a) the morphism's
  `F.op⋙G.op⋙(T₀⋙forget₂)` codomain defeq-elaborates against `(F⋙G).op⋙?R`, and (b) the
  `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal` instance resolves (its pattern is `?F.op ⋙ (?R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`, which
  the un-pinned composite morphism does NOT match).
- **δ/μ adjunction rewrites need `erw`, not `rw`**: `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal χ` (the registered
  instance) is defeq-but-not-syntactic to `aχ.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` (what the Mathlib lemmas use)
  → `rw [Adjunction.unit_app_tensor_comp_map_δ (adj := …)]` fails "pattern not found"; `erw` fires.
- **Namespaces**: `Adjunction.unit_app_tensor_comp_map_δ`, `Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv`
  are under `CategoryTheory.Adjunction`; **`conjugateEquiv` and `unit_conjugateEquiv` are BARE**
  (`CategoryTheory`, no `Adjunction.`).
- **`pushforwardComp = Iso.refl`** ⇒ `conjugateEquiv aC aχ (pullbackComp φ ψ).inv = 𝟙` (prove by
  `simp only [PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp, conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv,
  PresheafOfModules.pushforwardComp, Iso.refl_hom]`). The MATE step then drops the `pc.hom` factor.
- **Duplicate Category/MonoidalCategory instance wall**: generic `rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app]`,
  `rw [NatTrans.comp_app]`, `rw [MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_comp_tensorHom]`, `rw [Category.assoc]`
  ALL fail "pattern not found" on the goal's `≫`/`⊗ₘ` (the goal uses `monoidalCategory`'s Category
  vs the base `instCategory`). FIXES that worked: `erw [(iso).inv_hom_id_app P]` (explicit dot-notation
  term); pin `tensorHom_comp_tensorHom (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (..⋙forget₂))` + `erw`;
  `erw [← reassoc_of% (hmate P)]` for unit/map reassociation; `conv_lhs => rw [← htri M, ← htri N]`
  (project-stated lemmas DO `rw`-match); the trailing `(f≫g)≫h = f≫(g≫h)` closed by
  `exact Category.assoc _ _ _`.
- **NAME TRAP**: `Functor.map_id` resolves to the monad/`<$>` lemma; use `CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id`
  for `F.map (𝟙 X) = 𝟙 (F.obj X)`.
- **`reassoc_of% h` blows heartbeats** when wrapped in `erw` over `tensorHom_comp_tensorHom`; prefer
  plain `erw [← tensorHom_comp_tensorHom (C := …)]` then `exact Category.assoc _ _ _`.

## Next step
Close `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` in `mathlib-build`/fine-grained mode (the ModuleCat base-change
coherence, ~150 LOC; the `ext W x` leaf is in place). The moment it lands, `pullbackComp_δ` is fully
axiom-clean and Sq2 of `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` is discharged; then the 4-square assembly
(Sq1 `sheafificationCompPullback`-comp, Sq3 `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`, Sq4 `pullbackValIso`-comp +
`toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile`) closes `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`.

## Blueprint markers
- `lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange` (= `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`): still has a `sorry`; do NOT
  `\leanok`. The Sq2b sub-result is now formalized as `pullbackComp_δ` (proven modulo
  `pushforwardComp_lax_μ`). The plan agent may wish to add a `\lean{…}` hint for `pullbackComp_δ` /
  `pushforwardComp_lax_μ` in the Sq2/Sq2b paragraph and note that the residual is the
  "pushforwardComp-is-monoidal" ModuleCat coherence (NOT rfl, correcting the bw258-d3 sketch).

content: I'll start by reading my role and task context.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

62	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
63	
64	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
65	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
66	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
67	
68	namespace PresheafOfModules
69	
70	open InternalHom Opposite
71	
72	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
73	
74	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
75	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
76	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
77	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
78	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
79	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
80	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
81	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
82	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
83	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
84	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
85	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
86	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
87	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
89	  exact
90	    { toFun := fun φ =>
91	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
92	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
93	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
94	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
95	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
96	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
97	      invFun := fun r =>
98	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
99	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
100	      left_inv := fun φ => by
101	        ext Y
102	        dsimp only
103	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
104	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
105	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
106	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
107	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
108	        rfl
109	      right_inv := fun r => by
110	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
111	            (restr X.unop
112	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
113	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
114	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
115	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
116	        exact mul_one r }
117	
118	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
119	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
120	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
121	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
122	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
123	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
124	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
125	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
126	    (fun {X Y} f => by
127	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
128	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
129	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
130	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
131	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
132	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
133	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
134	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
135	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
136	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
137	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
138	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
139	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
140	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
141	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
142	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
143	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
144	      rw [hrm] at key
145	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
146	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
147	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
148	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
149	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
150	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
151	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
152	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
153	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
154	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
155	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
156	
157	end PresheafOfModules

165	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
166	
167	open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
168	/-- **Leg (A)∘(B): the sectionwise slice transport of the dual along an open immersion.**
169	
170	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X`, `M : X.Modules`, and an open `V` of `Y` (as `(Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`),
171	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
172	of `dual_restrict_iso`:
173	```
174	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
175	```
176	where `β` is the open-immersion structure ring morphism `Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙
177	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).
178	
179	The construction mirrors `homLocalSection` (the thin-poset `eqToHom`-conjugation slice transport)
180	composed with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear codomain-unit ring swap of leg
181	(B)): a dual section `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over (fV)` is reindexed across
182	`f.opensFunctor` to a dual section over `Over V`, conjugating each component by the structure ring
183	iso `f.appIso`; naturality on the thin poset `Opens Y` is `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
184	noncomputable def sliceDualTransport {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
185	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ) :
186	    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
187	      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
188	    letI β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
189	      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
190	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)).obj V) ≅
191	      ((PresheafOfModules.dual ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V) := by
192	  -- CONSTRUCTION PLAN (homLocalSection-style leg (A) ∘ restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv leg (B)):
193	  --
194	  -- Write `fV := f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`.  By `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_obj`,
195	  --   LHS carrier `L = (dual M.val).obj (op fV) = (restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X)`,
196	  --     a `𝒪_X(fV)`-module restricted along `β.app V : 𝒪_Y(V) ⟶ 𝒪_X(fV)` to a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module;
197	  --   RHS carrier `Rr = (restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V.unop 𝟙_Y)`,
198	  --     a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module via `internalHomObjModule`.
199	  --
200	  -- Build a `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear equivalence `e : L ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)] Rr` and return `e.toModuleIso`.
201	  --
202	  -- `e.toFun φ` (for `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X`) is the dual section over `Over V`
203	  -- whose component at `W : (Over V.unop)ᵒᵖ` (so `W' := W.unop.left ≤ V.unop`, with image
204	  -- `fW' := f.opensFunctor.obj W'`) is
205	  --   `(restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj W  ≃defeq  M.val.obj (op fW')`
206	  --     --[ φ.app (op (Over.mk (f.opensFunctor.map W.unop.hom))) ]-->  X.ring(fW')
207	  --     --[ (f.appIso W').hom : 𝒪_X(fW') ≅ 𝒪_Y(W') ]-->  Y.ring(W')  =  (restr V.unop 𝟙_Y).obj W,
208	  -- packaged as a `ModuleCat` hom over `𝒪_Y(W')`.  Naturality of this family in `W` is automatic
209	  -- on the thin poset `Opens Y` (`Subsingleton.elim` on the base maps, exactly as in
210	  -- `homLocalSection`'s `naturality` field).  `e.invFun` is the same with `(f.appIso W').inv` and
211	  -- the inverse reindexing (every `W'' ≤ fV` is `f.opensFunctor.obj (f⁻¹ᵁ W'')` since
212	  -- `fV ⊆ range f`); `left_inv`/`right_inv` collapse by `Iso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id` of `f.appIso`
213	  -- plus the down-set bijection `image_preimage_of_le`.  `𝒪_Y(V)`-linearity (`map_smul'`) is the
214	  -- `globalSMul`/`homModule`-action compatibility (post-composition with the structure scalar),
215	  -- intertwined by the ring iso — the presheaf-level shadow of `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`'s
216	  -- `map_smul'`.
217	  --
218	  -- The single load-bearing sub-build is `e.toFun`'s underlying `PresheafOfModules.Hom`; it is a
219	  -- structural copy of `homLocalSection` (component conjugation by `eqToHom` + the `f.appIso` ring
220	  -- iso) and of `dualPrecompEquiv` (the `≃ₗ` packaging).
221	  --
222	  -- STATUS (iter-258, HELD): the `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`/`PresheafInternalHom.lean` dependencies
223	  -- compile cleanly (this file builds with only the two typed sorries here), so the earlier
224	  -- "broken dependency" note is retired.  An empirical probe confirms
225	  -- `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso ?_` cleanly reduces this goal to a `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear equivalence
226	  --   `(restr fV' M.val ⟶ restr fV' 𝟙_X)  ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)]`
227	  --   `  (restr V ((pushforward β) M.val) ⟶ restr V 𝟙_Y)`
228	  -- (the ModuleCat instances synthesize; `fV' = f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`).  That `≃ₗ` is the
229	  -- **per-open shadow of `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence`**, i.e. the sheaf-level shared
230	  -- root `Scheme.Modules.overEquivalence` (`Picard/SheafOverEquivalence.lean`) localized to one
231	  -- open `V`.  Per the iter-258 plan this lane is HELD until that root lands; the closing move is
232	  -- then to obtain this iso as a *consumer* of `overEquivalence` (a one-liner), rather than
233	  -- hand-rolling the ~200 LOC sectionwise dual-section reindexing here.  Typed `sorry` retained at
234	  -- the intended signature.
235	  sorry

238	
239	Blueprint `lem:dual_restrict_iso` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_bridge`).  For an open immersion
240	`f : Y ⟶ X` and `M : X.Modules`, there is a canonical isomorphism of `𝒪_Y`-modules
241	```

243	```
244	natural in `M`, between the restriction of the sheaf-level dual and the dual of the
245	restriction.

316	noncomputable def dual_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)

321	  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
322	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
323	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
324	  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
325	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)

333	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=

336	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
337	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
338	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
339	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
340	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_

352	/-- **Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit.**

354	Local supplement (the `PresheafOfModules`-level ingredient of `dual_unit_iso`). -/
355	noncomputable def presheafDualUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :

438	/-- **The local section of the hom-sheaf manufactured from `f i`** (the load-bearing piece
439	of `homOfLocalCompat`, blueprint `localSection`).  Working with the underlying `Ab`-presheaves
440	`F = M.val.presheaf`, `G = N.val.presheaf`, the presheaf of types
441	`presheafHom F G` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) sends an open `W` to the morphisms of
442	the restrictions of `F`, `G` to the slice `Over W`.  Its value at `U i` is built from the
443	components of `f i`, conjugated by `eqToHom` along the down-set identity
444	`(U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` (valid for `V ≤ U i`).  The naturality field — the genuine
445	coherence risk — is automatic on the thin poset `Opens X` once the `eqToHom`-conjugation is
446	peeled, via `Subsingleton.elim` on the hom-sets. -/
447	noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
448	    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
449	    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
450	  app W :=
451	    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
452	    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
453	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
454	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
455	    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
456	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
457	      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
458	  naturality := by
459	    intro A B φ
460	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
461	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
462	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
463	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
464	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
465	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
466	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
467	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
468	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop B).hom.le
469	    -- naturality of the underlying ab-presheaf morphism of `f i`
470	    have hm := ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).naturality κ.op
471	    -- the two thin-poset square edges agree (`Opens X` is a thin poset)
472	    have hsubM : ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm)
473	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm) ≫ ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op :=
474	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
475	    have hsubN : ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB)
476	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA) ≫ ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op :=
477	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
478	    -- M-side: the φ-restriction followed by the `eqToHom` is the `eqToHom` followed by `κ`
479	    have hML : M.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫
480	          M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm))
481	        = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm)) ≫
482	          (M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op := by
483	      rw [(M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]
484	      exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
485	    -- N-side analogue
486	    have hNR : N.val.presheaf.map ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫
487	          N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB))
488	        = N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA)) ≫
489	          N.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op := by
490	      rw [(N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubN]
491	      exact N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
492	    dsimp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.op_map, Functor.op_obj, Functor.comp_obj]
493	    rw [← Category.assoc, hML]
494	    erw [Category.assoc, reassoc_of% hm, hNR]
495	    simp only [Category.assoc]
496	    rfl

147	       congr 1 }⟩

153	    ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left = U.ι ''ᵁ W := by

173	       rw [← (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map_comp, ← (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map_comp]
174	       congr 1
175	       exact (Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans

1	# Analogy: closing the `dual_restrict_iso` Step-4 residual — inverse-uniqueness vs. slice Beck–Chevalley
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment

11	
12	## Question
13	Two precise questions about the sole residual `sorry` of `dual_restrict_iso`
14	(`DualInverse.lean:259`), the PresheafOfModules-level iso
15	`(pushforward β).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)`:
16	
17	1. **Cheaper alternative** — can `dual_restrict_iso` be derived from the CLOSED
18	   `tensorObj_restrict_iso` via *uniqueness of monoidal inverses* (dual = ⊗-inverse), using a
19	   Mathlib "right duals are unique" / "strong monoidal functor preserves chosen right duals"
20	   idiom, WITHOUT a registered `MonoidalCategory` + rigid/`MonoidalClosed` structure on
21	   `PresheafOfModules`?
22	2. **If leg (A) is unavoidable** — the cleanest Mathlib idiom for the slice Beck–Chevalley
23	   `restr V ((pushforward β) M.val)` over `Over V ⊂ Opens Y` ↔ `restr fV M.val` over
24	   `Over fV ⊂ Opens X` transported across `f.opensFunctor`, and the minimal `sliceDualTransport`
25	   skeleton.

27	## Project artifact(s)
28	- `DualInverse.lean:233-259` — `dual_restrict_iso`; Steps 1–3 + H1 closed, one `sorry` at the Step-4 presheaf residual.
29	- `DualInverse.lean:358-407` — `homLocalSection`; the existing axiom-clean thin-poset `eqToHom`-conjugation slice transport (the structural template for leg A).
30	- `DualInverse.lean:439-442` — `image_preimage_of_le`; the down-set identity `W.ι ''ᵁ (W.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` powering the `eqToHom`-conjugations.
31	- `PresheafInternalHom.lean:234-266` — `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the leg-B scalar atom; CLOSED).
32	- `PresheafInternalHom.lean:658-674` / `893-896` — `restr` (= `pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)`), `dual M = internalHom M 𝟙_`; the slice-Hom value is NOT a single linear dual.
33	- `Vestigial.lean:617-718` — `overSliceSheafEquiv = (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr A`; the Sheaf/fixed-value-cat version proven INAPPLICABLE here.

35	## Decisions identified
36	
37	### Decision 1: Route (1) — derive `dual_restrict_iso` from `tensorObj_restrict_iso` by inverse-uniqueness
38	
39	- **Mathlib idiom (the shape exists)**: `CategoryTheory.hasRightDualOfEquivalence`
40	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Rigid.OfEquivalence`) — transports a right dual along a
41	  strong-monoidal equivalence; `CategoryTheory.rightDualIso`
42	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Rigid.Basic`) — `ExactPairing X Y₁ → ExactPairing X Y₂ → Y₁ ≅ Y₂`,
43	  the canonical uniqueness of right duals. Both are the *correct abstract statements* of
44	  "duals/inverses are unique and preserved".
45	- **Why it is NOT applicable** — `hasRightDualOfEquivalence`'s signature requires, simultaneously:
46	  1. `[MonoidalCategory C]` and `[MonoidalCategory D]`. **ABSENT**: `loogle "MonoidalCategory
47	     (PresheafOfModules ?R)"` → no results; `loogle "MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules ?R)"` →
48	     no results. The project carries its monoidal structure entirely by hand
49	     (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`, `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj`) precisely because
50	     no `MonoidalCategory` instance exists to register a `HasRightDual`/`ExactPairing` against.
51	  2. `[F.Monoidal]` — F (restriction along the open immersion) must be **strong** monoidal.
52	     The project only has it as **lax/oplax** (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, the oplax `δ` of
53	     `pullback`); the comparison `δ` is NOT an iso in general (this is the recurring wall of the
54	     whole tensor lane). Not strong.
55	  3. `[F.IsEquivalence]` — F must be an **equivalence**. Restriction along a non-surjective open
56	     immersion is a localization, **not** an equivalence of module categories. Fails outright.
57	  4. `HasRightDual X` / `ExactPairing M (dual M)` — `dual M = internalHom(M, 𝟙_)` is a
58	     categorical right dual only when `M` is **dualizable** (locally free of rank 1). It supplies
59	     an evaluation (`internalHomEval`) but **no coevaluation** `𝟙_ → dual M ⊗ M` and no zig-zag
60	     for general `M`. Crucially `dual_restrict_iso` is stated for **general `M`**, where the
61	     inverse-uniqueness argument is not even mathematically valid.
62	- **Even restricting to the consumer's invertible `L`** (`dual_isLocallyTrivial` uses
63	  `dual_restrict_iso U.ι L` on a line bundle): you would still have to *register* a
64	  `MonoidalCategory` instance, *build* the coevaluation, *verify* the two zig-zag identities to
65	  get `ExactPairing L (dual L)`, and *promote* restriction to a strong-monoidal equivalence —
66	  strictly **more** infrastructure than leg (A), not less.
67	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong as a shortcut — the rigid machinery needs four pieces of structure
68	  the project does not have and (for general M) cannot have.
69	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** (route 1 not viable — commit to leg A).
70	
71	### Decision 2: Route (2) — the cleanest atom for the leg-(A) slice transport
72	
73	- **Mathlib idiom (the engine)**: `CategoryTheory.Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv`
74	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.FullyFaithful`): `F.FullyFaithful → ((X ⟶ Y) ≃ (F.obj X ⟶ F.obj Y))`.
75	  This is the categorical bijection-of-Hom-sets that underlies leg (A): the slice value
76	  `restr V P ⟶ restr V Q` over `Over V` corresponds to `restr fV P' ⟶ restr fV Q'` over
77	  `Over fV` once the slice categories are identified by the fully faithful `f.opensFunctor`.
78	- **Mathlib idiom (the off-the-shelf slice equivalence)**: `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence`
79	  (`Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Over`): `Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. The slice `Over V ⊂ Opens Y` is
80	  identified with `Over fV ⊂ Opens X` by composing two `overEquivalence`s through the
81	  open-immersion homeomorphism `↥V ≅ ↥(fV)`. **Caveat** (loogle): every structure map of
82	  `overEquivalence` (unit/counit components) is an `eqToHom` — so invoking the full equivalence
83	  drags in exactly the `eqToHom`-bookkeeping you would otherwise write directly.
84	- **The leg-(B) scalar atom**: `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (project, CLOSED) is the correct
85	  ring-iso reconciliation of the codomain unit `𝒪_X(fV) ↝ 𝒪_Y(V)` via `β_V = (f.appIso V).inv`
86	  — confirmed by dual252; it realizes the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linearity at the terminal slice section.
87	- **Project-aligned recommendation**: because `Opens X`/`Over V` are **thin posets**, leg (A)
88	  collapses to exactly the construction the project ALREADY ships axiom-clean in this very file —
89	  `homLocalSection` (`DualInverse.lean:358`): conjugate the slice-Hom components by `eqToHom`
90	  along the down-set identity `image_preimage_of_le` (`DualInverse.lean:439`), with **naturality
91	  by `Subsingleton.elim`**, wrapped in `PresheafOfModules.isoMk` (mirroring `dualUnitIsoGen` /
92	  `dualIsoOfIso`). `Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv` and `overEquivalence` are the conceptual
93	  atoms, but in the thin setting they reduce to the `eqToHom`-conjugation the project can already
94	  drive. Do NOT build a heavyweight `Over V ≌ Over fV` equivalence object.
95	- **Gap**: the leg-(A) `≃ₗ` (`sliceDualTransport`) is a genuine new build, but a *down-set
96	  restriction of the open immersion's order-embedding*, not a missing Mathlib import — and
97	  structurally a copy of `homLocalSection`.
98	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** (build `sliceDualTransport` à la `homLocalSection`,
99	  compose with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`).
100	
101	## Recommendation
102	
103	**Drop route (1) decisively.** `hasRightDualOfEquivalence` / `rightDualIso` are the right abstract
104	statements but require four structures the project lacks: a `MonoidalCategory` instance on
105	`PresheafOfModules` (absent), a *strong* (not lax/oplax) monoidal restriction functor, restriction
106	being an *equivalence* (false for a non-surjective open immersion), and an `ExactPairing M (dual M)`
107	(only for dualizable M, whereas the lemma is general). Even for the invertible-`L` consumer this is
108	strictly more work than leg (A). Commit to leg (A).
109	
110	**Build leg (A) as `sliceDualTransport`, mirroring `homLocalSection`.** Minimal skeleton:
111	
112	```
113	-- β_V : 𝒪_Y(V) ≃+* 𝒪_X(fV)  (the open-immersion structure ring iso, = (f.appIso V).inv as RingEquiv)
114	-- LHS module = restrictScalars β_V applied to the slice-Hom over Over fV
115	-- RHS module = the slice-Hom over Over V
116	noncomputable def sliceDualTransport {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
117	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (Opens Y)ᵒᵖ) :
118	    (restr (f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop) M.val ⟶ restr (f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop) 𝟙_X)
119	      ≃ₗ[(𝒪_Y(V.unop) : Type u)]
120	    (restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V.unop 𝟙_Y) :=
121	  -- (A) Hom-set transport along the slice order-iso induced by f.opensFunctor:
122	  --     realized by eqToHom-conjugation of the φ.app components along
123	  --     image_preimage_of_le (f reflects ≤ on the down-set of fV), naturality = Subsingleton.elim;
124	  -- (B) ∘ restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv β_V  (the 𝒪_Y(V)-linear codomain-unit ring swap).
125	  ...
126	
127	-- Step-4 residual then closes by:
128	PresheafOfModules.isoMk
129	  (fun V => (sliceDualTransport f M V).toModuleIso)
130	  (fun {V W} g => /- thin poset Opens Y: Subsingleton.elim, as in dualUnitIsoGen/dualIsoOfIso -/)
131	```
132	
133	The load-bearing piece is `sliceDualTransport` itself (leg A); the outer `isoMk` naturality is
134	thin-poset-trivial. Build and verify `sliceDualTransport` as a standalone axiom-clean `def` FIRST
135	(it is a near-copy of `homLocalSection`'s `eqToHom`-conjugation pattern + `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`),
136	then assemble the residual. Atoms to import/cite: `Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv`
137	(`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.FullyFaithful`), `image_preimage_of_le` (project, this file),
138	`restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (project, CLOSED), `PresheafOfModules.isoMk`. Do NOT route through
139	`overSliceSheafEquiv` (Sheaf / fixed-value-cat — proven inapplicable) nor build a full
140	`Over V ≌ Over fV` equivalence object.

1	# Analogy: a `SheafOfModules` equivalence from a site equivalence with a *varying* structure ring sheaf
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ana258-overeq
8	
9	## Iteration
10	258
11	
12	## Question
13	Does Mathlib already have a "sheaf-of-modules equivalence induced by a site equivalence +
14	ring-sheaf identification" primitive (so the project should mirror it rather than build a
15	parallel API), and what is the minimal shape + construction skeleton for
16	`SheafOfModules.overEquivalence X U : SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf ≌ SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`?
17	
18	## Project artifact(s)
19	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundleCoherence.lean:203-206` — `chartOverIso` (the open `sorry`).
20	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean:291-323` — `dual_restrict_iso`
21	  / `sliceDualTransport` / `exists_tensorObj_inverse` consumer.
22	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/Vestigial.lean:689-721` — `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`
23	  (project's already-built dense-subsite datum) + `overSliceSheafEquiv` (the **fixed-value-cat**,
24	  ring-invariant version that is INAPPLICABLE here).
25	
26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision 1: parallel API vs. mirror an existing Mathlib primitive
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Cite:
31	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:305` (whole `section
32	  Equivalence`, L289–329). Signature (verbatim shape):
33	  ```
34	  variable {C} [Category C] {D} [Category D] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
35	    {K : GrothendieckTopology D} (eqv : C ≌ D)
36	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
37	    [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.functor J K] [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.inverse K J]
38	    (φ : S ⟶ (eqv.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J K).obj R)
39	    (ψ : R ⟶ (eqv.inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat K J).obj S)
40	    (H₁ …) (H₂ …)
41	  def pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence : SheafOfModules R ≌ SheafOfModules S
42	  ```
43	  This is **exactly** "equivalence of module-sheaf categories from an equivalence of sites + an
44	  iso of the two structure ring sheaves" — `S` and `R` are DIFFERENT sheaves of rings (on `C` resp.
45	  `D`), reconciled by `φ`/`ψ`. It is the Joël-Riou primitive that `QuasicoherentData.bind` uses
46	  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Quasicoherent.lean` ~L370). The underlying functor is
47	  `SheafOfModules.pushforward φ` (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:44`).
48	- **Project's current path**: `overSliceSheafEquiv` (`Vestigial.lean:715`) built via
49	  `Equivalence.sheafCongr` over `Functor.IsDenseSubsite` — but at a **fixed value category `A`**
50	  (`Sheaf … A`, not modules), so it CANNOT carry the varying base ring. Two prover sessions already
51	  confirmed it is inapplicable (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:637-663`; `DualInverse.lean:262-272`).
52	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost only if the project builds a *new* parallel equivalence by hand.
53	  Mathlib's `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` is the right tool and was simply not noticed.
54	- **Cost of divergence (if a hand-rolled equivalence is built)**: duplicate unit/counit/coherence
55	  proofs (~150+ LOC) that Mathlib already provides, plus a second incompatible "module-over-site"
56	  API that future quasi-coherent code can't consume.
57	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB — build via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`
58	  (no shipped divergent code yet, so this is an adopt-the-idiom, not a refactor).
59	
60	### Decision 2: full `Equivalence` vs. bare functor + two isos (minimal consumer shape)
61	
62	- **Mathlib idiom**: the documented Mathlib TODO (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean:19-22`) targets
63	  the **full** equivalence. The underlying functor alone is `SheafOfModules.pushforward φ`
64	  (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:44`); the `restrict ↦ over` natural iso is a `pushforwardComp` +
65	  `pushforwardNatIso` composite — and Mathlib *already ships this exact recipe* in
66	  `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:335-340`).
67	- **Project's current path**: directive proposes the full `Equivalence`.
68	- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent. The two consumers (`chartOverIso`, `sliceDualTransport`) need only
69	  `Φ.functor` applied to an iso `e`, plus two object isos (`restrict ↦ over`, `unit ↦ unit`). They
70	  do NOT use `Φ.inverse`/unit/counit. So a bare functor `restrictOverFunctor := pushforward φ`
71	  + the two isos is a strictly cheaper sufficient shape (skips `ψ`, `H₁`, `H₂`).
72	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (full equivalence is the canonical idiom and is only marginally more than the
73	  functor since continuity is free — see Decision 3 — but the functor-only `restrictOverIso` is a
74	  valid, documented fallback if `ψ`/`H₁`/`H₂` prove sticky).
75	
76	### Decision 3: the continuity prerequisites (the apparent blocker) are ALREADY in the project
77	
78	- **Mathlib idiom**: `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` needs `[IsContinuous eqv.functor J K]` AND
79	  `[IsContinuous eqv.inverse K J]`. Key chain:
80	  1. `Functor.IsDenseSubsite … ⇒ IsContinuous` is a `priority 900` instance
81	     (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/DenseSubsite/Basic.lean:548`).
82	  2. For an equivalence, `[e.inverse.IsDenseSubsite K J] ⇒ instance e.functor.IsDenseSubsite J K`
83	     **automatically** (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Equivalence.lean:106-108`).
84	- **Project's current path**: `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`
85	  (`Vestigial.lean:689`) is exactly `(overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite (gt ↥U) ((gt X).over U)`
86	  = `e.inverse.IsDenseSubsite K J` for `e = overEquivalence U`. So BOTH continuity legs resolve by
87	  typeclass inference; the `Over.lean:19-22` TODO is *already discharged by the project for this case*.
88	- **Gap**: identical (the project unknowingly built precisely the hypothesis Mathlib's auto-instance
89	  consumes).
90	- **Verdict**: PROCEED — no work needed for continuity.
91	
92	## Construction skeleton (confirmed primitives, file:line)
93	
94	Set `e := TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
95	(`Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean:41`), `C = Over U` with `J = (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U`,
96	`D = Opens ↥U` with `K = Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U`. Then
97	`SheafOfModules R ≌ SheafOfModules S` with `R = (↑U).ringCatSheaf` (on `D`), `S = X.ringCatSheaf.over U`
98	(on `C`) — i.e. the functor goes `SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf ⥤ SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`,
99	exactly the directive's `overEquivalence X U` orientation.
100	
101	1. Continuity: free (Decision 3). Bring `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite` into scope.
102	2. **The ring morphism `φ` (THE real content, ring-level, NOT module-level)**:
103	   `φ : (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ⟶ (e.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J K).obj (↑U).ringCatSheaf`.
104	   Sectionwise at `V : Over U` this is `O_X(V.left) ⟶ O_{↥U}(e.functor V)`, the structure sheaf of
105	   the open immersion `U.ι : ↥U ⟶ X`. Build from `U.ι.appIso`/`(U.ι.appIso _).inv` — the IDENTICAL
106	   datum `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor` uses (`Sheaf.lean:320`: `α U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv`).
107	   `ψ` is the symmetric inverse iso. `Scheme.ringCatSheaf` = `sheafCompose (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`
108	   applied to `X.sheaf` (`AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Presheaf.lean:34`).
109	3. `H₁`, `H₂`: equalities of ring-presheaf nat-transes. On the thin poset `Opens` *naturality* is
110	   `Subsingleton.elim`-free, but the hom equalities themselves are NOT subsingleton — they follow
111	   from `φ`/`ψ` being mutual inverses via the `appIso` round-trip + `Sheaf.hom_ext`/`ext`.
112	   (Skippable entirely in the functor-only shape, Decision 2.)
113	4. `overEquivalence X U := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence e φ ψ H₁ H₂`.
114	5. **Consumer iso (A) `restrict ↦ over`**: `(restrictFunctor U.ι ⋙ overEquivalence.functor).obj M ≅ M.over U`.
115	   `M.restrict U.ι` is *itself* `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `U.ι.opensFunctor`
116	   (`Sheaf.lean:319-322`); compose with `overEquivalence.functor = pushforward φ` via
117	   `SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp` (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:101`, it is `Iso.refl _`), then
118	   `pushforwardNatIso` (`:188`) along the `eqToIso` natural iso of the two underlying functors
119	   `Over U ⥤ Opens X` (both `V ↦ V.left`). This is a verbatim mirror of
120	   `restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso` (`Sheaf.lean:335-340`).
121	6. **Consumer iso (B) `unit ↦ unit`**: `overEquivalence.functor.obj (unit (↑U).ringCatSheaf) ≅
122	   unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` — `pushforward` of the unit module is the unit module up to the `φ`
123	   identification (the `pushforward`-of-unit computation; cf. the project's existing
124	   `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso` pattern).
125	7. `chartOverIso M U e := (A).symm ≪≫ overEquivalence.functor.mapIso e ≪≫ (B)`; the dual lane's
126	   `sliceDualTransport` consumes the same three pieces.
127	
128	## Recommendation
129	
130	Build `SheafOfModules.overEquivalence` by **instantiating
131	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` at `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U`** —
132	this is the canonical Mathlib idiom and the documented `Over.lean` TODO target. The apparent
133	"~200–350 LOC, Mathlib-scale" blocker collapses: (i) continuity is *already* discharged by the
134	project's `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite` + Mathlib's auto dense-subsite instance, and (ii) the only
135	genuine content — the ring morphism `φ` — is the open-immersion structure-sheaf iso the project
136	*already* writes inline in `restrictFunctor` (`(U.ι.appIso _).inv`). Realistic estimate **~120–220
137	LOC** in a standalone file, most of it mirroring `restrictFunctor` / `restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso` /
138	`pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. If `ψ`/`H₁`/`H₂` resist, fall back to the **functor-only**
139	`restrictOverFunctor := pushforward φ` + isos (A),(B): both consumers need only that, no inverse.
140	This is NOT a Mathlib gap-fill and NOT a parallel API — it is assembling three existing primitives.
